meta.title: tUnE-yArDs — Ear Clef
meta.description: Merrill Garbus builds whole songs live out of loop pedals, ukulele and her own voice — art-pop with the machinery on full display.

hero.name: tUnE-yArDs
hero.identity: Loop pedals, ukulele and a voice that builds whole songs from scratch, live — art-pop with the wiring showing.

featuredAlbums.title: w h o k i l l

story.heading: The Story
paragraphs: tUnE-yArDs is the project of Merrill Garbus, built around loop pedals, ukulele, lo-fi percussion and a voice that layers into its own choir in real time — with bassist Nate Brenner as her core collaborator. The stylized lowercase-and-capital name is part of the project's identity, right down to how it's written.

The 2009 debut BiRd-BrAiNs announced the method; w h o k i l l (2011) broke it wide open, folding funk, punk and pop into songs that stayed live-wire even on record. It was named the number one album of 2011 on The Village Voice's Pazz & Jop critics' poll and was later named among Pitchfork's 100 Best Albums of the Decade So Far.

Garbus and Brenner have kept moving — Nikki Nack (2014), I can feel you creep into my private life (2018), sketchy. (2021) and Better Dreaming (2025) — alongside side work scoring the 2018 film Sorry to Bother You.
Live, the appeal has never dimmed: watching Garbus construct a song from nothing, loop by loop, remains one of the most physically compelling things in current music.

items.name: Better Dreaming — Blue LP
items.description: The 2025 album on limited blue vinyl from the band's official Bandcamp.
